pages.full_text:
We spent the night at Branoe lodge next to St John's Church. On[?] Sunday
We drove to Westcale & walked up a very [?]path.
We went along tracks, some even, some [?] steep to a beautiful woodland path which took us back down to the Van. (Under 4 miles.) There was a motorbike scramble about to happen but we couldn't stay for it because we needed lunch. We passed the entrance & walking places to guide a few lead miners.
We returned to Killeyone to look at the machines. Some of the machines work like hob-pushing machines. 
We had lunch & came back to Grannie lodge again.
The rain held off for the morning. In the afternoon it was wet.
Monday
We went on a short walk from the nice campsite. There's a steep car ford, but the water is deep from the rains. (We slept with the skylight open & the rain didn't come in - v. pleasant.) We walked across the pretty stream using the footbridge.
We drove on to Lindbands -much deeded some our last visit. Most fascin ating are the 350 litres left over from the leaving staring.
pages.notes: Several words difficult to read due to handwriting speed/faintness. 'Branoe' may be 'Branwe' or similar; 'Westcale' uncertain; 'Killeyone' and 'Lindbands' are unclear readings. Phrase 'Most fascin ating are the 350 litres left over from the leaving staring' is particularly unclear - may be corrupted by page break or illegibility. No year directly evident on this page.

photo_captions: The most extraordinary of Widdecombe letters--the birthday pavilion from Canada sent A.D. 1900 with protective effects in letters--each in separate envelopes, the earliest saying a woman.
pages.notes: Right page is heavily obscured by a glued-in postcard/photo with green border. Much of the handwritten text on the right is partially obscured or cut off by the image. The caption text visible on the right side of the postcard is transcribed. 'Middle wend' may be 'Middle Wind' or another location. 'Brook House from Middle wend' is unclear phrasing - may be a place name. No clear year anchoring this section; '4th of July video' and date range '1906' are visible but context is unclear.
